summary: Needs changes: the initial Boundary Trace trigger wording drifted across the run contract, protocol and finding-validation template.

findings.title: Boundary Trace triggers are inconsistent across source artifacts
findings.why_it_matters: The run contract described broader trigger semantics than the template/protocol enforced, so acceptance could leave contradictory guidance in the finding-validation layer.
findings.recommendation: Align the protocol and finding-validation template trigger conditions with the run contract and strengthen the smoke check for those terms.
findings.blocker_path: Contract trigger semantics -> finding-validation template/protocol drift -> reviewer/fusion/finding validation may apply different Boundary Trace rules -> acceptance could preserve contradictory gate guidance.
findings.acceptance_impact: The slice would not reliably define when Boundary Trace is required.

self_declared_limitations: Reviewer did not run tests or validators.
